title: AP Computer Science A — Diagnostic Test

questions.text: What is printed when this code runs?
questions.explanation: Correct answer: A — "put". Concept: String.substring(start, end)

guiding_questions.text: What index does the character 'c' have in the string 'computer'?
If I call substring(a, b), which index is NOT included in the result?
Let's write out the index table together. What is index 6?

questions.explanation: Correct answer: B — 3.0. Concept: Integer division & type widening

guiding_questions.text: What data type is the result of 7 / 2 in Java before it's assigned to result?
If you wanted 3.5, what one change would you make to the code?
What would happen if you changed int y to double y? How does that change things?

questions.text: What is the value of the expression:   !(true && false) || (false || true)
questions.explanation: Correct answer: A — true. Concept: Boolean logic & operator precedence

guiding_questions.text: Walk me through each sub-expression step by step. Start with what's inside the parentheses.
What does the ! operator do to a boolean value?
What is !(false)? Now re-read the left side of the || operator.

questions.explanation: Correct answer: C — 2. Concept: Loop tracing with modulus

guiding_questions.text: What does the % operator compute? What is 4 % 2?
Can you trace through the loop and write down the value of i and count at each iteration?
Which numbers between 1 and 5 have no remainder when divided by 2?

questions.explanation: Correct answer: C — 3. Concept: Nested loop accumulation

guiding_questions.text: When i=1, what is the condition of the inner loop? How many times does it execute?
Make a table: for each value of i, how many times does sum increment?
When i=0, how many times does the inner loop run?

options: D) Compile error: area() is not static
questions.explanation: Correct answer: B — 20. Concept: Object instantiation & method calls

guiding_questions.text: What happens inside the constructor when we call new Rectangle(4, 5)?
After the constructor runs, what are the values of width and height?
When does the constructor run relative to when the object is used?

questions.text: Why are instance variables typically declared private in Java?
options: A) Private variables execute faster than public ones
B) To prevent the variable from ever being changed after the object is created
C) To enforce encapsulation — controlled access through public accessor/mutator methods
D) Java requires all instance variables to be private
questions.explanation: Correct answer: C — Encapsulation. Concept: Encapsulation / data hiding

guiding_questions.text: If a variable is private, can a method inside the same class access it?
What is the difference between private and final in Java?
If private meant 'can never be changed,' how would a setter method work?

questions.explanation: Correct answer: B — 9. Concept: Array traversal & max-finding

guiding_questions.text: What is the value of max after the loop processes arr[2]=8?
Does the loop check arr[4]=9? What happens to max at that point?
How many iterations does the loop run? What is arr.length for this array?
ArrayList has two remove methods. One takes an index, one takes an Object. Which is being called here?
After all three add() calls, what is the list? What are the indices of each element?
What index does the element 20 have in the list [10, 20, 30]?

questions.explanation: Correct answer: A — 6. Concept: 2D array row/column indexing

guiding_questions.text: In a 2D array, what does the first index represent? What does the second represent?
Can you list out all three rows of this grid?
What is grid[2][1]? How is that different from grid[1][2]?
